title.en: MARINE PROPULSION OR STEERING (propulsion of air-cushion vehicles B60V1/14; specially adapted for submarines, other than nuclear propulsion, B63G; specially adapted for torpedoes F42B19/00)
Propulsive elements directly acting on water (jet propulsion B63H11/00)
{using Magnus effect}
of rotary type
of non-rotary type
characterised solely by flotation properties, e.g. drums
Propeller-blade pitch changing {(aircraft propellers B64C11/30; rotors of turbines F01D7/00; axial wind motors F03D7/022; axial-flow pumps F04D29/00)}
{with individually adjustable blades}
{comprising means for locking blades in position}
{Detecting or transmitting propeller-blade pitch angle}
{characterised by self-adjusting pitch, e.g. by means of springs, centrifugal forces, hydrodynamic forces}
actuated by control element coaxial with propeller shaft, e.g. the control element being rotary {(B63H3/002 takes precedence, fluid actuated B63H3/081)}
characterised by use of non-mechanical actuating means, e.g. electrical (B63H3/002 takes precedence)
characterised by having pitch control conjoint with propulsion plant control
the pitch being adjustable only when propeller is stationary (B63H3/002 takes precedence)
Arrangements on vessels of propulsion elements directly acting on water
{Front propulsors, i.e. propellers, paddle wheels, or the like substantially arranged ahead of the vessels' midship section}
of paddle wheels, e.g. of stern wheels
of propellers (forming part of outboard units {or Z-drives} B63H20/00)
Propulsion directly actuated on air (jet propulsion B63H11/00)
using propellers
Sail or rigging arrangements specially adapted for water sports boards, e.g. for windsurfing or kitesurfing
Kite-sails; Kite-wings; Control thereof; Safety means therefor
Rigging arrangements involving masts, e.g. for windsurfing
Arrangements for improving or maintaining the aerodynamic profile of sails, e.g. cambers, battens or foil profiles
Accessories, e.g. repair kits or kite launching aids
Arrangements for handling, stowing or transport thereof
Marine propulsion provided directly by wind power (wind-motors driving underwater propulsive elements B63H13/00)
using Magnus effect
using sails or like wind-catching surfaces (sail or rigging arrangements specially adapted for water sports boards, e.g. for windsurfing or kitesurfing B63H8/00)
Marine propulsion by water jets
{using Coanda effect, i.e. the tendency of fluid jets to be attracted to nearby surfaces}
{using the eductor or injector pump principle, e.g. jets with by-pass fluid paths}
{with propulsive medium supplied from sources external to propelled vessel, e.g. water from public water supply}
{Arrangements of two or more jet units}
having means to prevent foreign material from clogging fluid passage way
the propulsive medium being ambient water
the propulsive medium being steam or other gas
Marine propulsion by wind motors driving water-engaging propulsive elements
Marine propulsion by use of vessel-mounted driving mechanisms co-operating with anchored chains or the like
Marine propulsion by muscle power
{used on vessels dynamically supported, or lifted out of the water by hydrofoils}
Movable thwarts; Footrests
Oars; Sculls; Paddles; Poles
Rowlocks; Mountings therefor
Other apparatus for converting muscle power into propulsive effort
Marine propulsion not otherwise provided for
by using energy derived from movement of ambient water, e.g. from rolling or pitching of vessels
by discharging gas into ambient water
by direct engagement with water-bed or ground
Outboard propulsion units, e.g. outboard motors or Z-drives; Arrangements thereof on vessels

{Arrangements, apparatus and methods for handling fluids used in outboard drives (for handling exhaust gas B63H20/24; for handling cooling-water B63H20/28; cooling outboard marine engines F01P3/202; air intakes for outboard marine engines F02M35/167)}
{Arrangements of two, or more outboard propulsion units}
{Arrangements of two or more propellers, or the like on single outboard propulsion units}
{Trolling propulsion units (trolling plates for slowing down B63H25/50; dynamo-electric machines of trolling units H02K)}
{Tools, specially adapted for maintenance, mounting, repair, or the like of outboard propulsion units, e.g. of outboard motors or Z-drives}
Mounting of propulsion units (B63H20/08 takes precedence)
Means enabling movement of the position of the propulsion element, e.g. for trim, tilt or steering; Control of trim or tilt (initiating means for steering B63H25/02)
Transmission between propulsion power unit and propulsion element
{Arrangements, apparatus and methods for handling exhaust gas in outboard drives, e.g.} exhaust gas outlets {(in engines, e.g. outboard marine engines, F01N)}
{Arrangements, apparatus and methods for handling cooling-water in outboard drives, e.g.} cooling-water intakes {(cooling circuits for outboard marine engines F01P3/202)}
Housings {(air intakes for outboard engines F02M35/167)}

Transporting or testing stands {(hand carts for transporting outboard units B62B; measuring torque G01L3/00, measuring thrust of propellers G01L5/133, testing in general G01M); Use of outboard propulsion units as pumps}; Protection of power legs {, e.g. when not in use}
Use of propulsion power plant or units on vessels
{the power plant using fuel cells for energy supply or accumulation, e.g. for buffering photovoltaic energy}
{the vessel being driven by hot gas positive-displacement engine plants of closed-cycle type, e.g. Stirling engines}
the vessels being steam-driven (B63H21/18 takes precedence)
the vessels being motor-driven (B63H21/175, B63H21/18 take precedence; {cooling circuits with liquid-to-liquid heat-exchange relative to marine vessels F01P3/207})
the vessel being powered by land vehicle supported by vessel
the vessels being powered by nuclear energy
the vessels being powered by combinations of different types of propulsion units
Control means for engine or transmission, specially adapted for use on marine vessels
the propulsion power units being controlled from exterior of engine room, e.g. from navigation bridge; Arrangements of order telegraphs
{the vessels being small craft, e.g. racing boats}
Mounting of propulsion plant or unit, e.g. for anti-vibration purposes (hull reinforcements therefor B63B3/70)
Arrangements of propulsion power-unit exhaust uptakes; Funnels peculiar to vessels
Covers or casing arranged to protect plant or unit from marine environment

Apparatus or methods specially adapted for use on marine vessels, for handling power plant or unit liquids, e.g. lubricants, coolants, fuels or the like ({in outboard drives B63H20/001; } lubricating or cooling machines or engines in general F01 - F04)

Transmitting power from propulsion power plant to propulsive elements (adaptation of transmission to allow adjustment in direction of propellers B63H5/125; transmission between wind motors and propulsive elements B63H13/00; in outboard propulsion units B63H20/14; adaptation of transmission to allow adjustment of location of propellers B63H20/08)
{using a drive acting on the periphery of a rotating propulsive element, e.g. on a dented circumferential ring on a propeller, or a propeller acting as rotor of an electric motor}
with mechanical gearing
with non-mechanical gearing
with synchronisation of propulsive elements
characterised by use of clutches
Other parts

Steering; Slowing-down otherwise than by use of propulsive elements (using movably-installed outboard propulsion units B63H20/00); Dynamic anchoring, i.e. positioning vessels by means of main or auxiliary propulsive elements
{Steering specially adapted for towing trains, tug-barge systems, or the like; Equipment or accessories therefor}
Initiating means for steering {, for slowing down, otherwise than by use of propulsive elements, or for dynamic anchoring}
Steering by rudders (by rudders carrying propellers B63H25/42)
Steering or dynamic anchoring by propulsive elements (by jets B63H25/46); Steering or dynamic anchoring by propellers used therefor only; Steering or dynamic anchoring by rudders carrying propellers
Steering or slowing-down by extensible flaps or the like
Steering or dynamic anchoring by jets {or by rudders carrying jets (steering or dynamic anchoring by deflecting or directing main propulsion jets B63H11/00)}
Steering or slowing-down by deflection of propeller slipstream otherwise than by rudder
Slowing-down means not otherwise provided for
Parts for steering not otherwise provided for
